subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: The comparative focus and three thematic threads are stated clearly in the title and introduction, providing a solid purpose.
The research question is precise, appropriately narrow, and consistently connected to analytical sections.
weaknesses: Methodology remains largely implicit, with minimal discussion of secondary source selection.
Occasional narrative summary interrupts analytical clarity and drift from thesis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates the topic accurately and maintains focus via thematic headings, aligning with the upper half of the 3â4 band. The research question is effectively integrated, but omissions in an explicit methods section and occasional plot summary drifts prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Demonstrates clear understanding of both primary texts with well-chosen quotations.
Uses literary terminology routinely and usually accurately to support analysis.
weaknesses: Secondary sources are acknowledged but seldom analysed, limiting deeper contextual integration.
Occasional imprecision in terminology (e.g., misuse of âoxymoronâ) detracts from consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good textual knowledge and mostly accurate use of subject-specific terms, placing the work solidly in the 3â4 band. Lack of sustained synthesis of secondary scholarship and rare terminological slips preclude a top-band mark.

strengths: Primary-text research is consistently relevant and demonstrates informed source choice.
Analysis often links stylistic features to thematic claims, yielding supported conclusions.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis fluctuates, with some paragraphs relying on summary and unsupported assertions.
Evaluation is present but limited; the conclusion lacks critical reflection on limitations or alternative readings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and relevance fall within the 4â6 band, but uneven analytical depth and only partial critical evaluation justify a lower-band mark. The argument is coherent yet does not consistently achieve the well-supported, critically evaluative standard of higher bands.

strengths: Structure follows standard EE conventions with logical headings that mirror the analytical framework.
Title page, word count, and reference list are present, aiding basic navigation.
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers, undermining navigability.
Pagination and formatting inconsistencies (hyperlink breaks, spacing) detract from overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the structure is clearly appropriate, execution flaws in the table of contents and formatting issues place presentation in the upper half of the 1â2 band rather than the top band.

strandSelections.reasoning: Method is mostly implicit: close reading of primary texts supplemented by a short reference list of secondary criticism.  While quotations from the novels are frequent and apt, supporting scholarship is cited only sparingly and, in some cases, links are incomplete.  There is evidence of informed source selection, yet range and integration are moderate rather than extensive, matching the mid-band (3-4).

Basic layout requirementsâtitle, word count, reference listâare met, but hyperlinks break across lines, page numbers are not visible in the extract, and some spacing/typing inconsistencies occur.  Most but not all formatting conventions are applied correctly, warranting the 1-2 band with a score at the upper end.

Primary-text research is extensive and always relevant; secondary research is limited yet still connected to the RQ.  Citations come from peer-reviewed journals and interviews, indicating conscious source choice.  Because breadth and integration of scholarship are uneven, quality is âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ, placing the work comfortably in the 4-6 band.

The research topic and the three thematic threads are stated explicitly in the title and reiterated with purpose in the introduction; the student keeps the comparative lens in view throughout the body sections headed âLoss and Destruction,â âNostalgia,â and âSex and Desire.â  Transitions occasionally lapse into plot summary, yet the reader is never uncertain about what is being examined or why.  Overall clarity is solid rather than exceptional, aligning best with the upper end of the 3-4 band.

An argument is presentâboth authors show how loss leads to growthâbut evaluative moments (e.g., weighing Murakamiâs versus Isherwoodâs treatment of nostalgia) are brief.  The conclusion synthesises main findings yet offers little critical reflection on limitations or alternative readings.  Development is therefore good with some evaluative insight, aligning with the lower half of the 4-6 band.

Analysis moves beyond description by linking stylistic features (e.g., listing, narrative voice, symbolism) to thematic claims, but depth fluctuates.  Some paragraphs offer insightful commentary on character psychology; others drift into paraphrase.  Conclusions are usually supported by evidence, though warrants are occasionally asserted rather than demonstrated, indicating solid but not consistently strong analytical control.

The essay follows EE conventionsâtitle page, table of contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion and references.  Headings mirror the analytical framework, aiding navigation.  However, the table of contents is incomplete and pagination is unclear in places, so while structure is clearly appropriate, execution is not flawless, suiting a score of 3 in the top band.

The student demonstrates good textual knowledge, referencing key plot points, characters and authorial techniques from both novels and embedding numerous primary quotations.  Secondary materials are acknowledged but seldom analysed, limiting coherence of wider literary context.  This represents clear understanding but not the sustained, well-synthesised mastery required for the top band.

Literary termsâmetaphor, asyndeton, motif, oxymoron, personification, foilâare employed routinely and usually accurately to support points.  Occasional mis-labelling (e.g., âchain of oxymoronsâ where oppositions are not strictly oxymoronic) shows some imprecision, but overall usage is mostly sound and appropriate for SL level, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The research question is quoted verbatim, is suitably narrow (two primary texts, three themes) and directs the essayâs structure.  Each analytical section returns to at least one of the named themes, demonstrating a sustained connection between question and discussion.  Minor drift into narrative retelling prevents a perfect score, but overall focus is strong and clearly appropriate for an English A EE.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the comparative focus and introduces the three thematic threads, establishing a precise research scope with purpose and focus.
Although the research question is verbatim and focused, consider refining wording to reduce narrative summary drift, which occasionally interrupts analytical clarity.
The incomplete pagination in the footer area undermines structural clarity. Ensure consistent page numbering throughout the document.
Word count is provided and stays within the 4,000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirements.
The table of contents lists sections but omits page numbers, making navigation less effective. Include corresponding page numbers for each heading.
This paragraph begins with narrative summary rather than analytical focus. Strengthen by linking examples directly back to the research question.
Quotation is effective, but analysis of the syndetic listing could be deepened by explaining its psychological impact on Toru.
The introduction effectively outlines thematic threads and contextualises both novels, demonstrating solid knowledge of subject and sources.
The methodology is implied rather than explicit. Consider adding a brief methods section to clarify choice of primary close-reading and secondary scholarship.
Repeated use of summary in the Loss and Destruction section weakens argument development. Emphasise critical evaluation over plot recounting.
The metaphor âdeath took [him] as wellâ is aptly identified but could benefit from secondary critical support to reinforce interpretation.
The label âchain of oxymoronsâ is inaccurate here. These are paradoxical juxtapositions rather than true oxymora. Precision in terminology is essential.
Analysis of the house as extended metaphor for Georgeâs psyche is insightful; consider connecting this directly to the theme of memory for cohesion.
The section on Nostalgia integrates one secondary source (Searles, 2012) but does not critically evaluate its argument. Expand on its relevance.
Good use of story-within-a-story concept; however, clarify how this structure reflects Toruâs psychological state in relation to memory.
The distinction between Murakamiâs focus on past and Isherwoodâs on present is noted, but deeper synthesis in the conclusion would strengthen the evaluation.
Hyperlinks in the references break across lines and URLs are inconsistently formatted, reducing readability. Apply consistent citation styling and hyperlink settings.
An OCR artifact â\overline{7}â appears in the footer. Remove or correct this to maintain professional formatting.
This paragraph offers rich insight into nostalgiaâs effects, yet occasionally lapses into summary. Emphasise critical evaluation of key quotations.
The conclusion summarizes findings but lacks reflection on limitations or alternative interpretations. Include evaluative comments for depth.
Sections are logically ordered, yet transitions between themes sometimes revert to narrative recap. Strengthen coherence with clearer topic sentences.
Reference entries lack uniformity in punctuation and author-date formatting. Adopt a single style guide (e.g., APA) and apply it consistently.
Secondary sources are cited but rarely integrated into the analytical argument. Aim to discuss how criticsâ perspectives support or challenge your claims.
In the âSex and Desireâ section, some interpretations rely heavily on summary. Strengthen analysis by connecting sexual imagery directly to psychological themes.
The foil analysis between Toru and Nagasawa is insightful; consider briefly contrasting this with George and Kenny to reinforce comparative lens.
Engaging insights on oceanic imagery are presented, yet the analytical focus drifts. Tie metaphoric interpretation back to the theme of love or memory explicitly.
Transitions to Isherwoodâs analysis sometimes lack clear link back to the thesis. Use connective phrases to maintain comparative focus.
Headings are well chosen to reflect thematic structure; ensure consistent formatting (font size, spacing) to enhance presentation.
The reference list correctly includes primary texts and criticism, but URLs are overly long. Use DOI or shortened links for readability.
The essay demonstrates clear command of literary terminology, though occasional imprecision occurs (e.g., âoxymoronâ misuse). Review concepts for accuracy.
The introduction skillfully sets context, but consider briefly previewing secondary sources to clarify methodology and scholarly dialogue.
Spacing inconsistencies occur between paragraphs, particularly in the Body sections. Apply uniform paragraph spacing to enhance readability.
The introductionâs closing sentence restates the research question effectively; consider reframing it as an analytical thesis statement.
Consider adding more recent criticism to broaden scholarly perspectives, particularly on gender and sexuality in Isherwoodâs work.
